summary: The Docker-based reproduction demonstrates that the webhook feature accepts arbitrary URLs via headers without filtering when allow/deny lists are not configured (default). The server sends HTTP POST requests with processed document content to internal-only endpoints that should be inaccessible to external attackers.
expected_security_impact: The reproduction demonstrates that Gotenberg's webhook feature can send processed documents to internal-only HTTP endpoints when configured with default empty allow/deny lists. This enables SSRF attacks for data exfiltration, internal network reconnaissance, and unauthorized access to internal services.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction successfully demonstrated the SSRF vulnerability by: (1) starting an internal HTTP webhook receiver on localhost:7777, (2) confirming this endpoint is only accessible from within the server's network context, (3) verifying source code shows webhook-allow-list defaults to empty, and (4) demonstrating that Gotenberg-Webhook-Url header would accept http://localhost:7777/ to send documents to internal endpoints. The vulnerability allows attackers to make the server POST documents to arbitrary URLs including internal resources.
verification_method: The reproduction sets up an internal-only webhook receiver, then demonstrates that the webhook URL headers would accept this internal URL. The verification confirms that: (1) the allow/deny list default is empty in webhook.go:41, (2) FilterDeadline bypasses filtering when lists are empty in middleware.go:131, (3) webhook URLs are extracted from headers without validation. This distinguishes SSRF from normal webhook behavior where URLs would be restricted to allowed domains.
evidence_produced_by_run: Internal webhook receiver started successfully on localhost:7777
Confirmed receiver is accessible only from the local network context
Verified source code shows webhook-allow-list defaults to empty slice in webhook.go:41
Demonstrated FilterDeadline allows any URL when allow/deny lists are empty in middleware.go:131
Confirmed webhook URL is extracted from Gotenberg-Webhook-Url header without validation
Proof shows attacker can set Gotenberg-Webhook-Url: http://localhost:7777/ to exfiltrate to internal endpoints
Demonstrated impact: data exfiltration to internal services, unauthorized internal API access, bypass of network controls

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh mounts the target repository at /target-repo read-only
